photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Night photo. Ground floor entirely covered in black paint with large-scale graffiti art. Upper floor has white/cream cladding. Heavily altered but underlying house-form with gable still legible.
photo_observations.window_details: one small window visible on upper floor (left side), ground floor has storefront glazing behind security gate
photo_observations.door_details: commercial entrance at ground level, heavily graffitied surround
photo_observations.cornice_details: yellow/green decorative trim band at roofline with zigzag pattern
photo_observations.decorative_details: yellow/green zigzag trim at roofline, large-scale graffiti mural covering entire ground floor facade
photo_observations.storefront_description: Black-painted ground floor with small storefront window. Security gate visible. Building number 75 painted in yellow.
photo_observations.signage_observed: Number 75 in yellow. Large-scale graffiti art covering ground floor.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: entire facade re-clad, ground floor painted black with graffiti, original Victorian details completely obscured, upper floor covered in white stucco/cladding
photo_observations.notes: matches_hcd corrected from false to true. Style corrected from Vernacular to Victorian. Despite heavy street-level alterations (black paint, graffiti), the house-form with gable roof is still legible. The semi-detached pair form is consistent with the 1904-1913 construction date.

notes: nighttime photo; building is heavily altered from its original Victorian semi-detached form; adjacent to white stucco-clad building on left; the number 75 is painted in yellow at ground level

facade_description: 75 Kensington Ave presents a house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a black painted ground floor with massive graffiti mural, white/grey stucco-clad upper floor, yellow/green decorative trim at roofline palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade is organized as a storefront base with 1 upper-window bay, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as an early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects architectural styles of the period. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructe
